title: armeria rouyana subsp. littorea
intro: Armeria rouyana subsp. littorea, also known as littoral sea thrift, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae. It is a perennial herb that thrives in coastal areas with moist soil, characterized by its pink or white flowers and narrow, grass-like leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Armeria rouyana subsp. littorea can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide health benefits like relieving congestion and soothing respiratory tract irritations.
